summary: Base64-decoded payload evaluated at load time
details: router_init.js line 4 contains the pattern `eval(Buffer.from(<base64>, 'base64').toString(...))` — a base64-encoded blob is decoded and passed directly to eval at module load time. This is a standard obfuscation technique used to hide malicious behavior from casual review and from registry scanners; whatever the encoded blob contains will execute with the privileges of any process that loads this file. The pattern has no legitimate use in a published library: real code is shipped as readable JavaScript or as transparently minified output, not as base64 fed to eval. Anyone who installs this package and loads router_init.js (directly or via require chains) runs the hidden payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
